present_usage.summary: Able to convince by reasons, benefits, or appeal.

senses.definition: Able to convince by reasons, benefits, or appeal.

examples.text: The word persuasive changes what claims the agent may safely make.

cultural_frame.summary: persuasive appears in communication where tone, risk, evidence, or commitment must be controlled.

frames.label: semantic control
frames.description: Frames persuasive as a term whose pragmatic force can change the safe output path.

pragmatics.summary: Persuasive language can help decision-making when transparent about benefits and tradeoffs.

expansion.paragraph: persuasive is represented here as a semantic-control term: its present usage and pragmatic force help decide whether an agent should ask, check evidence, avoid overclaiming, or revise before export.
